volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Self-Tormentor (Section 2.3.321b - Section 2.3.380c)

sections.label: Section 2.3.321b
sections.translation: Upon my faith , he ought to have a hearing . ( To CLITIPHO .) Do be silent .

sections.label: Section 2.3.322
sections.original: Vis amare ; vis potiri ; vis quod des illi effici :
sections.translation: You wish to indulge in your amours ; you wish to possess your mistress ; you wish that to be procured wherewithal to make her presents ; in getting this , you do not wish the risk to be your own . You are not wise to no purpose ,— if indeed it is being wise to wish for that which can not happen .

sections.label: Section 2.3.325
sections.original: Aut haec cum illis sunt habenda , aut illa cum his mittenda sunt .
sections.translation: Either the one must be had with the other , or the one must be let alone with the other . Now , of these two alternatives , consider which one you would prefer ; although this project which I have formed , I know to be both a wise and a safe one . For there is an opportunity for your mistress to be with you at your father ’ s house , without fear of a discovery ; besides , by these self-same means , I shall find the money which you have promised her —

sections.label: Section 2.3.330
sections.original: Quod ut efficerem orando surdas iam aures reddideras mihi .
sections.translation: to effect which , you have already made my ears deaf with entreating me . What would you have more ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.331b
sections.translation: If , indeed , this could be brought about —

sections.label: Section 2.3.331c
sections.translation: If , indeed ? You shall know it by experience .

sections.label: Section 2.3.332
sections.original: Age , age , cedo istuc tuum consilium : quid id est ?
sections.translation: Well , well , disclose this project of yours . What is it ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.332b
sections.translation: We will pretend that your mistress is his .

sections.label: Section 2.3.333b
sections.original: Pulchre : cedo quid hic faciet sua ?
sections.translation: Very fine ! Tell me , what is he to do with his own ? Is she , too , to be called his , as if one was not a sufficient discredit ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.335
sections.translation: No — she shall be taken to your mother .

sections.label: Section 2.3.335b
sections.translation: Why there ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.335c
sections.translation: It would be tedious , Clitipho , if I were to tell you why I do so ; I have a good reason .

sections.label: Section 2.3.336b
sections.translation: Stuff ! I see no grounds sufficiently solid why it should be for my advantage to incur this risk . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.3.338
sections.original: Mane . Habeo aliud , si istud metuis ; quod ambo confiteamini
sections.translation: Stay ; if there is this risk , I have another project , which you must both confess to be free from danger .

sections.label: Section 2.3.339b
sections.translation: Find out something of that description , I beseech you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.339c
sections.translation: By all means ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.340
sections.original: Ibo obviam hinc : dicam ut revertantur domum .
sections.translation: I ’ ll go meet her , and tell her to return home .

sections.label: Section 2.3.340b
sections.translation: Ha ! what was it you said ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.341b
sections.original: Ademtum tibi iam faxo omnem metum ,
sections.translation: I ’ ll rid you at once of all fears , so that you may sleep at your ease upon either ear . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.3.343
sections.translation: What am I to do now ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.343b
sections.translation: What are you to do ? The goods that —

sections.label: Section 2.3.343c
sections.translation: Only tell me the truth , Syrus .

sections.label: Section 2.3.344b
sections.original: Age modo , hodie sero ac nequicquam voles .
sections.translation: Dispatch quickly ; you ’ ll be wishing just now too late and in vain .

sections.label: Section 2.3.345
sections.original: Datur : fruare dum licet , nam nescias —
sections.translation: The Gods provide , enjoy while yet you may ; for you know not —

sections.label: Section 2.3.346
sections.translation: Syrus , I say !

sections.label: Section 2.3.346b
sections.translation: Go on ; I shall still do that which I said . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.3.347
sections.original: Eius sit potestas posthac an nunquam tibi .
sections.translation: Whether you may have another opportunity hereafter or ever again .

sections.label: Section 2.3.348
sections.original: Verum hercle istuc est . Syre , Syre inquam , heus , heus , Syre .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , that ’ s true . ( Calling .) Syrus , Syrus , I say , harkye , harkye , Syrus !

sections.label: Section 2.3.349
sections.translation: He warms a little . ( To CLITIPHO .) What is it you want ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.349b
sections.translation: Come back , come back .

sections.label: Section 2.3.349c
sections.translation: Here I am ; tell me what you would have . You ’ ll be presently saying that this , too , doesn ’ t please you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.350b
sections.translation: Nay , Syrus , I commit myself , and my love , and my reputation entirely to you : you are the seducer ; take care you don ’ t deserve any blame .

sections.label: Section 2.3.353
sections.original: Ridiculum est te istuc me admonere , Clitipho ;
sections.translation: It is ridiculous for you to give me that caution , Clitipho , as if my interest was less at stake in this affair than yours .

sections.label: Section 2.3.355
sections.original: Hic si quid nobis forte adversi evenerit ,
sections.translation: Here , if any ill luck should perchance befall us , words will be in readiness for you , but for this individual blows ( pointing to himself ) . For that reason , this matter is by no means to be neglected on my part : but do prevail upon him ( pointing to CLINIA ) to pretend that she is his own mistress .

sections.label: Section 2.3.358b
sections.translation: You may rest assured I ’ ll do so . The matter has now come to that pass , that it is a case of necessity .

sections.label: Section 2.3.360b
sections.translation: ’ Tis with good reason that I love you , Clinia .

sections.label: Section 2.3.361
sections.translation: But she mustn ’ t be tripping at all .

sections.label: Section 2.3.361b
sections.translation: She is thoroughly tutored in her part .

sections.label: Section 2.3.362
sections.original: At hoc demiror , qui tam facile potueris
sections.translation: But this I wonder at , how you could so easily prevail upon her , who is wont to treat such great peoplel [*] with scorn .

sections.label: Section 2.3.364
sections.original: In tempore ad eam veni , quod rerum omnium est
sections.translation: I came to her at the proper moment , which in all things is of the first importance :

sections.label: Section 2.3.365
sections.original: Primum : nam quendam misere offendi militem
sections.translation: for there I found a certain wretched captain soliciting her favors : she artfully managed the man , so as to inflame his eager passions by denial ; and this , too , that it might be especially pleasing to yourself . But hark you , take care , will you , not to be imprudently impetuous .

sections.label: Section 2.3.370
sections.original: Patrem novisti ad has res quam sit perspicax :
sections.translation: You know your father , how quick-sighted he is in these matters ; and I know you , how unable you are to command yourself . Keep clear of words of double meaning , [*] your sidelong looks , sighing , hemming , coughing , tittering .

sections.label: Section 2.3.374
sections.translation: You shall have to commend me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.374b
sections.translation: Take care of that , please .

sections.label: Section 2.3.374c
sections.translation: You yourself shall be surprised at me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.375
sections.original: Sed quam cito sunt consecutae mulieres !
sections.translation: But how quickly the ladies have come up with us !

sections.label: Section 2.3.376
sections.translation: Where are they ? ( SYRUS stands before him .) Why do you hold me back ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.376b
sections.original: Iam nunc haec non est tua .
sections.translation: For the present she is nothing to you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.377
sections.original: Scio ; apud patrem : at nunc interim .
sections.translation: I know it , before my father ; but now in the mean time —

sections.label: Section 2.3.377b
sections.translation: Not a bit the more .

sections.label: Section 2.3.378
sections.translation: Do let me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.378b
sections.translation: I will not let you , I tell you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.378c
sections.translation: But only for a moment , pray .

sections.label: Section 2.3.378d
sections.translation: I forbid it .

sections.label: Section 2.3.379
sections.translation: Only to salute her .

sections.label: Section 2.3.379b
sections.translation: If you are wise , get you gone .

sections.label: Section 2.3.379c
sections.translation: I ’ m off .

sections.label: Section 2.3.380
sections.translation: But what ’ s he to do ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.380b
sections.translation: He will stay here .

sections.label: Section 2.3.380c
sections.translation: O happy man !
